preview_text:
The model assigns nearly identical winning chances to both sides, with the Netherlands at 34.1% and Japan at 34.4%, making this a competitive pairing at a neutral venue. The underdog designation is negligible; both teams sit within a fraction of a percentage point in the model's assessment.

The draw emerges as a meaningful outcome in the model's forecast, weighted at 31.6% — close enough to either team's win probability that a stalemate ranks as a plausible result. This distribution suggests a tightly matched contest with no clear favorite, and considerable uncertainty across all three outcomes.

The market consensus diverges noticeably from the model. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both favor the Netherlands more decisively, crediting them with roughly 47% win probability against Japan's 26%. That represents a gap of approximately 12 percentage points in the Netherlands' favor compared to the model's assessment. The market also assigns the draw lower weight — around 27% — than the model does. The dashboard does not claim to know which source better reflects the true match dynamics.
